element_introduction_order.element: Initial small army and controlled towns
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player begins with starter forces to establish foothold
Balance of power scale; tipping control toward player's side
Critical survival resource; can be obtained from towns, sheep, bartering, or enemy pillaging
Subordinate captains with lag-time commands; player can only control armies equal to captain count
Towns produce items; strategic importance of resource-specific towns; automatic equipment distribution hierarchy
Large-scale deforestation affects weather patterns, slowing movement; limited terrain effects
Surviving neutral captains come under player command; expands available armies
Killing opposing captains tips power balance; required to win maps

typical_relief_triggers: Recruiting new soldiers from conquered settlement
Acquiring food supplies to sustain army
typical_tension_triggers: Lag-time delay on distant captain commands
Insufficient manpower or equipment for larger towns
Multiple opposing captains coordinating against player
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Asynchronous real-time with command lag creating delayed gratification; tension builds through resource scarcity and enemy pressure; relief via incremental conquest and recruitment

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: 3D topography influences movement speed; deforested areas become slower due to weather changes

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low-stakes conquest with small initial army; simple food and manpower acquisition from nearby towns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased complexity through multi-army management, captain lag-time mechanics, and strategic equipment sourcing from specialized towns
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complete map conquest requiring balance of power tipping; management of multiple distant captains; coordinating food supply chains across territory
notable_spikes: First encounter with larger enemy-held towns requiring coordinated multi-army assault
Managing multiple captains with significant command lag across large map distances
Food supply crises during territorial expansion phases

notable_level_patterns.description: Maps present starting position with small army and few controlled towns; victory requires progressively conquering all or most remaining towns while maintaining resource balance and eliminating enemy captains
Certain towns are critical for producing specific equipment; players must secure and maintain control of these strategically important settlements
Subordinate captain effectiveness decreases with distance; lag time forces strategic consideration of army placement and communication bottlenecks
